name: Flax Vegan Egg Substitute
directions: Simmer flax seeds and water in a saucepan for about 5 minutes (or less or more time depending on the amount you are making) or until thick, egg-like consistency has been reached.
Let cool before using in a recipe.** (About 10 minutes).

This recipe makes 1 eggs worth, but it can be easily made into a larger quantity using the 1:3 ratio- for example, 1 cup ground flax seeds and 3 cups water (that would make 16 eggs worth) and will keep in the fridge for about 2 weeks.
Use about 1/4 cup (which is equal to 4Tbs) egg substitute for every egg.

similar.name: My Favorite Vegan French Toast Recipe

name: Arame Salsa
directions: Soak the arame in water for 20 minutes.
Drain, then place in a medium skillet with the apple cider.
Simmer, uncovered, until the cider has completely evaporated, about 10 minutes.
Transfer the arame to a bowl and let it sit for a few minutes to cool.
Add other ingredients and mix thoroughly.

similar.name: My Favorite Vegan French Toast Recipe

name: Vegan Tofu-Powered "sour Cream"
directions: Combine all ingredients in food processor. Process until smooth.
Refrigerate 2+ hours to blend flavors.
Store in refrigerator for up to 2 weeks.

similar.name: My Favorite Vegan French Toast Recipe

name: Basic Whole Wheat Pasta Dough
directions: Put it into a mixer with bread hooks or a food processor, and knead until really smooth.
I suppose you could also do this by hand, but I haven't tried it!
We've also substituted 1/4 of the flour with kamut, buckwheat etc. to add extra flavor, and the recipe suggested that chopped spinach, herbs, or tomato paste could be added.
From astels@fsd.cpsc.ucalgary.ca (astels).
Posted to MM-Recipes Digest V4 #169 by Nancy Miller <millern@redwood.cc.andrews.edu> on Jul 1, 1997.

similar.name: My Favorite Vegan French Toast Recipe

name: Parsley Pesto With Sun-Dried Tomatoes
directions: In a food processor, place parsley and pulse briefly.
Add sun dried tomatoes, pine nuts, garlic, lemon juice and salt, then pulse until almost smooth.
Drizzle in olive oil and pulse again briefly to incorporate.
Serve with pasta, sesame crackers or walnut crackers.

similar.name: My Favorite Vegan French Toast Recipe

name: Whole Wheat Pie Shells
directions: Combine the flour and salt in a large mixing bowl.

Gradually add the butter, while stirring with a fork. Still stirring with the fork add the ice water, alittle at a time, until the dough is dampened enough to form a ball.Divide the dough into 3 balls.(Each ball will make one pie shell or 1 cobbler).
Save the balls you don't need and freeze them for another day.To use the frozen dough, let it stand at room tempature until thoroughly defrosted.
Roll out the dough (can be rolled out between 2 pieces of wax paper) Place the dough in a buttered pie pan. Poke holes in the pie shell before using.

similar.name: My Favorite Vegan French Toast Recipe

name: Easy Tomato Florentine Soup (Vegan Too!)
directions: Cook orzo according to package (I recommend slightly under cooking), drain and set aside.
Add tomato soup to a pot with cannellini beans and cook until heated through. Add spinach and orzo and cook until spinach is wilted. Remove from heat and serve.

name: My Favorite Vegan French Toast Recipe
directions: Mix all dry ingredients together thoroughly.
Add lemon juice to soymilk and allow for it to sour for five minutes(makes a vegan buttermilk).
Mix wet into dry ingredients.
Soak each bread(stale, fresh, or oven dried)side for one minute each.

Heat pan to medium, coat with 1 teaspoon or so of Earth Balance. Cook pan side for 1 minute and meanwhile dust face-up side with cinnamon. Then flip the toast a little prematurely, so that you can "unstick" and dust. I flip and cook each side, a total of two times each.
Yummy.
